threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Dalston Junction' instead of just 'the Junction'. Old hands know better than to be so formal around here.
Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading a pigeon on the 35 last night. The bird didn't seem impressed, but he carried on like it was sold-out Wembley.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the old lady down the street offered me her homemade pickles while gossiping about the latest BVG strike. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a street vendor selling currywurst next to an open-air techno party at 11 AM on a Sunday. That’s a Sunday ritual!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re locals when they know to check BART status before leaving—everyone else just assumes it’ll run on time. Good luck with that.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton yesterday, and a guy serenaded us with a ukulele. Only in the Tenderloin, right? At least he made the delay bearable.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hit the bars late, but many of us are home by 10 for family dinner. The nightlife is just a slice of Gràcia life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still look up at the buildings, mesmerized by the stained glass, while locals barely notice.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to the Overground as the 'train' and ask locals for directions to the nearest 'tube station.'
Last night on the 50, a guy serenaded a group with a ukulele; felt like a scene from a film, everyone joined in on 'Wonderwall.' Pure Brixton magic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing soccer in the rain, their laughter echoing off the damp streets, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a late-night Döner while a stray cat lounges on the counter, like it owns the place. It's just part of the vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can tell you which tacos to grab right after a Civic Center BART closure. The real ones always know the shortcuts.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a suit chase down a train at Powell, yelling 'Wait!' as the doors closed. Classic SF hustle. Muni is a stage.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think the fountains in Pl. del Sol are just for show, but locals actually gather there, especially during summer evenings, for unsanctioned concerts.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their unwavering excitement about the local boulangeries, completely missing the fact that the best croissants are at the corner café.
